behaviors.description: Embedding vector dimensionality matches the vector index dimension for a query.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at the model-output-to-index-schema handoff, not a single pure function. Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins (cross-module contract). Integration vs System -> Integration wins (no OS/subprocess behavior is inherently required). LLM-DEP/AI-DEP upgrade: behavior explicitly requires the real embedding model output shape; a stubbed embedding can hide model/config drift. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about dimension contract, not real network/OS failure semantics; Workflow rejected because only one agentic decision/output is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model for fixed queries, fetch actual index dimension from the real vector DB schema, and assert every produced vector length equals the index dimension before/at search insertion.

behaviors.description: Similarity search results are returned in descending cosine similarity order.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs can occur at DB engine/client boundary (metric config, score parsing, tie handling), not only in local sort logic. Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins (search stack wiring). Integration vs System -> System wins because real vector DB query execution/score behavior is part of the failure boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repo can always return correctly sorted fixtures and miss real engine/config errors. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because this behavior targets numeric score ordering from vector search, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Index known vectors in a real vector DB instance, run real similarity queries, and assert returned scores are monotonic non-increasing and correspond to descending cosine similarity under the configured metric.

behaviors.description: LLM re-ranking changes ordering for nuanced-intent queries versus raw similarity ranking.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the core failure is whether model judgment on nuanced intent actually reorders candidates. Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins (reranker orchestration with candidate list). Integration vs System -> Integration wins (real OS behavior is not the core boundary). LLM-DEP upgrade: correctness depends on real LLM output quality and can vary run-to-run; stubs deterministically hide failures. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because this behavior validates a single agentic decision point (rerank step), not a multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: For a curated nuanced query set, run real retrieval then real LLM reranking, and assert at least one top-K position changes from raw similarity order with stability checks over repeated runs (allowing bounded variance).

behaviors.description: Queries with no close matches return an empty set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: true negatives depend on embedding-space semantics plus thresholding; local stubs can mask irrelevant-but-near outputs. Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins (embedding/search/threshold handoff). Integration vs System -> Integration wins (no specific OS/subprocess condition defines failure). LLM-DEP/AI-DEP upgrade: behavior quality depends on real model-produced embeddings and relevance calibration; deterministic fake embeddings can trivially pass. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because this can be decided without requiring multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real embeddings and real vector search over a corpus with out-of-domain queries known to have no semantic neighbors; assert final API response is empty and no item exceeds the configured relevance threshold.

behaviors.description: Median end-to-end query latency (embed + search + rerank + response) remains below 2 seconds.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: latency budget failures emerge across chained stages, not a single module. Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins (cross-module pipeline). Integration vs System -> System wins (real network/service latency is essential). MULTI-STEP upgrade: behavior explicitly covers multiple agentic steps in sequence (embedding model call and LLM rerank), so minimum boundary is Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because single-model timing misses compounded pipeline effects stated in behavior text ('end-to-end ... re-ranked results').
behaviors.test_description: Run the real API in a production-like environment with real embedding and LLM providers plus real vector DB, execute a representative query load, and assert p50 end-to-end latency is <2.0s over the sample window.
